forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8e80'
+project: ai-panel
+title: Initialize the board folder as a SwissArmyHammer workspace (inline sah init)
+---
+## What
+When a board folder is opened in the kanban app it should be a full SwissArmyHammer workspace â€” skills, prompts, the SAH directory â€” so the in-process agent has the same toolset and skills `sah` provides. Run the `sah init` workspace-setup logic **in-process** (Rust), never by shelling out to `sah init`.
+
+- `sah init` is composable `Initializable` components: `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/install/init.rs` builds an `swissarmyhammer_common::lifecycle::InitRegistry`, registers components via `swissarmyhammer_cli::commands::registry::register_all`, and runs `run_all_init(&scope, &reporter)`.
+- In the kanban-app, on board open (`AppState::open_board` / `BoardHandle::open`, `apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs`), run the same init for the board folder at project scope: build an `InitRegistry`, register the components, run init rooted at the board directory. Must be idempotent â€” safe to run on every open.
+- Reach the init logic as a library: depend on `swissarmyhammer-cli` (it has a `lib.rs`) and call `register_all`, OR â€” if that CLI dependency is too heavy â€” first extract the init registry + `Initializable` components into a library crate. Decide during implementation and document the choice.
+- Scope decision: include the workspace + skills components; EXCLUDE the Claude-Code `.claude/settings.json` bits (`install_deny_bash`, `install_statusline`) â€” those configure the external `claude` CLI and are out of scope for the kanban-app's in-process agent.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Opening a board folder makes it a SAH workspace (SAH directory + skills present) via in-process init â€” no `sah` subprocess.
+- [ ] Init is idempotent across repeated board opens.
+- [ ] The chosen way to reach the init logic (CLI-as-library vs extracted crate) is documented in the task.
+- [ ] `cargo build -p kanban-app` is clean.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Integration test: open a board in a fresh temp dir; assert the SAH workspace layout and skills are created; open again, assert idempotent (no error, no duplication).
+- [ ] `cargo test -p kanban-app` is green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write the workspace-created + idempotency tests first.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -25,4 +25,32 @@
 - [ ] `cargo test -p kanban-app` is green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` â€” write the workspace-created + idempotency tests first.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write the workspace-created + idempotency tests first.
+
+## BLOCKED â€” Implementation paused for guidance (2026-05-16)
+
+Research is complete; implementation is blocked on two genuine deviations from the plan that require a decision before any code is written. Per the `implement` skill ("DO NOT deviate from the plan -- if you run into a problem, you need to stop and ask the user for guidance"), stopping here.
+
+### Finding 1 â€” The init components are CWD-rooted, not parameterized by a root directory
+The plan says "build an InitRegistry ... run init rooted at the board directory." The `Initializable` components cannot be rooted at an arbitrary directory via a parameter. They read the process working directory / git root directly:
+- `ProjectStructure::init` -> `SwissarmyhammerDirectory::from_git_root()` else `std::env::current_dir()`.
+- `SkillDeployment` -> `mirdan::install::deploy_skill_to_agents`, which writes to `mirdan::store::skill_store_dir(false)` = the CWD-relative `.skills/` and symlinks into CWD-relative agent dirs (`.claude/skills/`, etc.).
+- `ClaudeMd::init` -> `swissarmyhammer_common::utils::find_git_repository_root()`.
+- Lockfile load/save -> `std::env::current_dir()`.
+
+The only way to "root" them at the board folder is to temporarily set the process CWD (the documented `CurrentDirGuard` RAII pattern; the CLI's own `registry.rs` tests do exactly this). But changing global process CWD inside a running Tauri desktop app â€” even briefly â€” races every other thread: the filesystem watcher, other open boards, concurrent Tauri commands. That is materially different from, and riskier than, what the plan envisions.
+
+### Finding 2 â€” `SkillDeployment` targets detected coding-agent dirs, not the board folder
+`SkillDeployment` deploys builtin skills into *detected coding-agent* directories (`.claude/skills/` etc.) via mirdan's global agent detection â€” not into a self-contained `.skills/` under the board folder that the kanban-app's in-process agent consumes. "Skills present in the board folder" is not what this component straightforwardly produces.
+
+### Library-access analysis (the delegated CLI-vs-crate choice)
+- `register_all` IS reachable as a library API: `swissarmyhammer-cli` has a `lib.rs` with `pub mod commands`, so `swissarmyhammer_cli::commands::registry::register_all` is public.
+- BUT depending on `swissarmyhammer-cli` drags in its full dependency tree into `kanban-app`: `llama-agent` (llama.cpp + Metal C++ build), `git2`, `rmcp`, `reqwest`, `swissarmyhammer-doctor`, `swissarmyhammer-lsp`, `swissarmyhammer-tools`. That is a large, inappropriate compile-time and binary-size burden for the desktop app.
+- Extracting the registry + components into a new library crate is a sizable refactor of CLI-private modules (`commands::registry`, `commands::install::components`, `commands::skill`) and still does not solve Finding 1 (the components remain CWD-rooted).
+
+### Decision needed from the user â€” pick one
+A. Add `swissarmyhammer-cli` as a dependency of `kanban-app` (accept the heavy dep tree), drive init by briefly setting process CWD to the board folder under a `CurrentDirGuard` + a global lock; accept the GUI-CWD-mutation race risk; only run the workspace + skills components (skip Claude-Code settings components).
+B. Extract the init registry + `Initializable` components into a new lightweight library crate, AND rework those components to accept an explicit root `&Path` instead of reading CWD/git-root. Larger scope; cleanest result; no CWD mutation. Likely needs its own kanban task.
+C. Narrow the kanban-app's in-process init to only what it actually needs â€” create the `.sah/` workspace dir + deploy builtin skills into a board-local `.skills/` â€” implemented directly against the lightweight `swissarmyhammer-common` (`SwissarmyhammerDirectory::from_custom_root`) and `swissarmyhammer-skills` (`SkillResolver`) crates, all rooted at an explicit board path with no CWD mutation and no heavy CLI dependency. Does not literally reuse `register_all`/the CLI components, but achieves the acceptance criteria (SAH dir + skills present, idempotent) cleanly.
+
+Recommendation: Option C. It satisfies every acceptance criterion, needs no process-CWD mutation, adds no heavy dependency, and uses crates the kanban-app can already reach. It does deviate from "reuse the exact `register_all` components," so it needs explicit approval before proceeding.
\ No newline at end of file

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -11,46 +11,72 @@

 - `sah init` is composable `Initializable` components: `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/install/init.rs` builds an `swissarmyhammer_common::lifecycle::InitRegistry`, registers components via `swissarmyhammer_cli::commands::registry::register_all`, and runs `run_all_init(&scope, &reporter)`.

Must be idempotent â€” safe to run on every open.

 ## Acceptance Criteria

+- [x] Integration test: open a board in a fresh temp dir; assert the SAH workspace layout and skills are created; open again, assert idempotent (no error, no duplication).
+- [x] `cargo test -p kanban-app` is green.

 ## Workflow
 - Use `/tdd` â€” write the workspace-created + idempotency tests first.

-## BLOCKED â€” Implementation paused for guidance (2026-05-16)
+## Implementation Notes (2026-05-16) â€” Option B chosen (per user decision)

-Research is complete; implementation is blocked on two genuine deviations from the plan that require a decision before any code is written. Per the `implement` skill ("DO NOT deviate from the plan -- if you run into a problem, you need to stop and ask the user for guidance"), stopping here.
+The prior agent's "BLOCKED" section is superseded. The user reviewed the A/B/C
+options and **directed Option B**: extract the init registry + workspace/skills
+components into a new lightweight library crate AND rework them to accept an
+explicit root `&Path` instead of relying on process CWD / git-root detection.
+No process-CWD mutation anywhere.
+
+### New crate: `swissarmyhammer-workspace-init`
+`crates/swissarmyhammer-workspace-init/` â€” a lightweight library (deps:
+`swissarmyhammer-common`, `-config`, `-prompts`, `-skills` only; no heavy CLI
+dependency tree). Public API:
+- `run_workspace_init(root: &Path, scope, reporter) -> Vec<InitResult>` â€” builds a fresh `InitRegistry`, registers the components, runs them.
+- `register_workspace_init(&mut InitRegistry, root: &Path)` â€” registers the components into a caller-owned registry.
+- Re-exports the lifecycle vocabulary (`InitRegistry`, `InitResult`, `InitScope`, `InitStatus`, `Initializable`).

-### Finding 1 â€” The init components are CWD-rooted, not parameterized by a root directory
-The plan says "build an InitRegistry ... run init rooted at the board directory." The `Initializable` components cannot be rooted at an arbitrary directory via a parameter. They read the process working directory / git root directly:
-- `ProjectStructure::init` -> `SwissarmyhammerDirectory::from_git_root()` else `std::env::current_dir()`.
-- `SkillDeployment` -> `mirdan::install::deploy_skill_to_agents`, which writes to `mirdan::store::skill_store_dir(false)` = the CWD-relative `.skills/` and symlinks into CWD-relative agent dirs (`.claude/skills/`, etc.).
-- `ClaudeMd::init` -> `swissarmyhammer_common::utils::find_git_repository_root()`.
-- Lockfile load/save -> `std::env::current_dir()`.
+### What was extracted and rerooted
+Two root-explicit `Initializable` components:
+- `ProjectStructure` (priority 20) â€” creates `<root>/.sah/` (+ `workflows/`) and `<root>/.prompts/` via `SwissarmyhammerDirectory::from_custom_root(root)`. No git-root detection, no `current_dir()`.
+- `SkillDeployment` (priority 30) â€” renders the builtin skills through the Liquid prompt engine and writes them to a board-local `<root>/.sah/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (plus bundled resources). It does NOT use mirdan agent-detection or a CWD-relative store â€” the workspace is self-contained for an in-process agent. Idempotent: each skill dir is recreated from scratch per run. Frontmatter formatting is shared via `swissarmyhammer_skills::deploy::format_skill_md`.

-The only way to "root" them at the board folder is to temporarily set the process CWD (the documented `CurrentDirGuard` RAII pattern; the CLI's own `registry.rs` tests do exactly this). But changing global process CWD inside a running Tauri desktop app â€” even briefly â€” races every other thread: the filesystem watcher, other open boards, concurrent Tauri commands. That is materially different from, and riskier than, what the plan envisions.
+The Claude-Code `.claude/settings.json` components (`deny-bash`, `statusline`) are intentionally EXCLUDED â€” out of scope.

-### Finding 2 â€” `SkillDeployment` targets detected coding-agent dirs, not the board folder
-`SkillDeployment` deploys builtin skills into *detected coding-agent* directories (`.claude/skills/` etc.) via mirdan's global agent detection â€” not into a self-contained `.skills/` under the board folder that the kanban-app's in-process agent consumes. "Skills present in the board folder" is not what this component straightforwardly produces.
+### CLI migration (init logic not forked)
+`apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/install/components/mod.rs`:
+`ProjectStructure::init` now resolves the root (git root, else CWD) and
+delegates the actual `.sah/` + `.prompts/` creation to
+`swissarmyhammer_workspace_init::ProjectStructure::new(root).init(...)`. The
+CLI keeps its own `deinit` (directory removal) and its mirdan-based
+`skill::SkillDeployment` (which targets detected coding-agent dirs â€” a distinct
+target from the workspace-local `.sah/skills/`, so not a fork). The `.sah/`
+workspace-structure logic now lives in exactly one place. CLI builds clean;
+all 451 CLI lib tests pass.

-### Library-access analysis (the delegated CLI-vs-crate choice)
-- `register_all` IS reachable as a library API: `swissarmyhammer-cli` has a `lib.rs` with `pub mod commands`, so `swissarmyhammer_cli::commands::registry::register_all` is public.
-- BUT depending on `swissarmyhammer-cli` drags in its full dependency tree into `kanban-app`: `llama-agent` (llama.cpp + Metal C++ build), `git2`, `rmcp`, `reqwest`, `swissarmyhammer-doctor`, `swissarmyhammer-lsp`, `swissarmyhammer-tools`. That is a large, inappropriate compile-time and binary-size burden for the desktop app.
-- Extracting the registry + components into a new library crate is a sizable refactor of CLI-private modules (`commands::registry`, `commands::install::components`, `commands::skill`) and still does not solve Finding 1 (the components remain CWD-rooted).
+### kanban-app wiring
+`apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs`: new `ensure_sah_workspace(&Path)` helper is
+called at the top of `BoardHandle::open` (reached from `AppState::open_board`).
+It roots `run_workspace_init` at the board folder (the parent of the `.kanban`
+directory), at `InitScope::Project`, on every board open. Idempotent; errors
+are logged and swallowed so a board still opens on a filesystem hiccup. The
+process CWD is never touched.

-### Decision needed from the user â€” pick one
-A. Add `swissarmyhammer-cli` as a dependency of `kanban-app` (accept the heavy dep tree), drive init by briefly setting process CWD to the board folder under a `CurrentDirGuard` + a global lock; accept the GUI-CWD-mutation race risk; only run the workspace + skills components (skip Claude-Code settings components).
-B. Extract the init registry + `Initializable` components into a new lightweight library crate, AND rework those components to accept an explicit root `&Path` instead of reading CWD/git-root. Larger scope; cleanest result; no CWD mutation. Likely needs its own kanban task.
-C. Narrow the kanban-app's in-process init to only what it actually needs â€” create the `.sah/` workspace dir + deploy builtin skills into a board-local `.skills/` â€” implemented directly against the lightweight `swissarmyhammer-common` (`SwissarmyhammerDirectory::from_custom_root`) and `swissarmyhammer-skills` (`SkillResolver`) crates, all rooted at an explicit board path with no CWD mutation and no heavy CLI dependency. Does not literally reuse `register_all`/the CLI components, but achieves the acceptance criteria (SAH dir + skills present, idempotent) cleanly.
+### Tests added
+`apps/kanban-app/tests/workspace_init.rs` â€” 3 integration tests (written first,
+TDD): workspace + skills created on open; repeated open is idempotent (no
+duplication); init does not mutate process CWD. Plus 10 unit tests + 1 doctest
+in the new crate.

-Recommendation: Option C. It satisfies every acceptance criterion, needs no process-CWD mutation, adds no heavy dependency, and uses crates the kanban-app can already reach. It does deviate from "reuse the exact `register_all` components," so it needs explicit approval before proceeding.
\ No newline at end of file
+### Verification (all green)
+- `cargo build -p kanban-app` â€” clean
+- `cargo test -p kanban-app` â€” 126 tests pass (incl. 3 new integration tests)
+- `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-workspace-init` â€” 11 tests pass
+- `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli --lib` â€” 451 tests pass
+- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` â€” clean, zero warnings
\ No newline at end of file

 - In the kanban-app, on board open (`AppState::open_board` / `BoardHandle::open`, `apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs`), run the same init for the board folder at project scope: build an `InitRegistry`, register the components, run init rooted at the board directory. -- [x] `cargo build -p kanban-app` is clean.
+- [ ] Opening a board folder makes it a SAH workspace (SAH directory + skills present) via in-process init â€” no `sah` subprocess.
+- [ ] Init is idempotent across repeated board opens.
+- [ ] The chosen way to reach the init logic (CLI-as-library vs extracted crate) is documented in the task.
+- [ ] `cargo build -p kanban-app` is clean.

 ## Tests
-- [x] Integration test: open a board in a fresh temp dir; assert the SAH workspace layout and skills are created; open again, assert idempotent (no error, no duplication).
-- [x] `cargo test -p kanban-app` is green.
+- [ ] Integration test: open a board in a fresh temp dir; assert the SAH workspace layout and skills are created; open again, assert idempotent (no error, no duplication).
+- [ ] `cargo test -p kanban-app` is green.

 ## Workflow
 - Use `/tdd` â€” write the workspace-created + idempotency tests first.

-## Implementation Notes (2026-05-16) â€” Option B chosen (per user decision)
+## BLOCKED â€” Implementation paused for guidance (2026-05-16)

-The prior agent's "BLOCKED" section is superseded. The user reviewed the A/B/C
-options and **directed Option B**: extract the init registry + workspace/skills
-components into a new lightweight library crate AND rework them to accept an
-explicit root `&Path` instead of relying on process CWD / git-root detection.
-No process-CWD mutation anywhere.
+Research is complete; implementation is blocked on two genuine deviations from the plan that require a decision before any code is written. Per the `implement` skill ("DO NOT deviate from the plan -- if you run into a problem, you need to stop and ask the user for guidance"), stopping here.

-### New crate: `swissarmyhammer-workspace-init`
-`crates/swissarmyhammer-workspace-init/` â€” a lightweight library (deps:
-`swissarmyhammer-common`, `-config`, `-prompts`, `-skills` only; no heavy CLI
-dependency tree). Public API:
-- `run_workspace_init(root: &Path, scope, reporter) -> Vec<InitResult>` â€” builds a fresh `InitRegistry`, registers the components, runs them.
-- `register_workspace_init(&mut InitRegistry, root: &Path)` â€” registers the components into a caller-owned registry.
-- Re-exports the lifecycle vocabulary (`InitRegistry`, `InitResult`, `InitScope`, `InitStatus`, `Initializable`).
+### Finding 1 â€” The init components are CWD-rooted, not parameterized by a root directory
+The plan says "build an InitRegistry ... run init rooted at the board directory." The `Initializable` components cannot be rooted at an arbitrary directory via a parameter. They read the process working directory / git root directly:
+- `ProjectStructure::init` -> `SwissarmyhammerDirectory::from_git_root()` else `std::env::current_dir()`.
+- `SkillDeployment` -> `mirdan::install::deploy_skill_to_agents`, which writes to `mirdan::store::skill_store_dir(false)` = the CWD-relative `.skills/` and symlinks into CWD-relative agent dirs (`.claude/skills/`, etc.).
+- `ClaudeMd::init` -> `swissarmyhammer_common::utils::find_git_repository_root()`.
+- Lockfile load/save -> `std::env::current_dir()`.

-### What was extracted and rerooted
-Two root-explicit `Initializable` components:
-- `ProjectStructure` (priority 20) â€” creates `<root>/.sah/` (+ `workflows/`) and `<root>/.prompts/` via `SwissarmyhammerDirectory::from_custom_root(root)`. No git-root detection, no `current_dir()`.
-- `SkillDeployment` (priority 30) â€” renders the builtin skills through the Liquid prompt engine and writes them to a board-local `<root>/.sah/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (plus bundled resources). It does NOT use mirdan agent-detection or a CWD-relative store â€” the workspace is self-contained for an in-process agent. Idempotent: each skill dir is recreated from scratch per run. Frontmatter formatting is shared via `swissarmyhammer_skills::deploy::format_skill_md`.
+The only way to "root" them at the board folder is to temporarily set the process CWD (the documented `CurrentDirGuard` RAII pattern; the CLI's own `registry.rs` tests do exactly this). But changing global process CWD inside a running Tauri desktop app â€” even briefly â€” races every other thread: the filesystem watcher, other open boards, concurrent Tauri commands. That is materially different from, and riskier than, what the plan envisions.

-The Claude-Code `.claude/settings.json` components (`deny-bash`, `statusline`) are intentionally EXCLUDED â€” out of scope.
-
-### CLI migration (init logic not forked)
-`apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/install/components/mod.rs`:
-`ProjectStructure::init` now resolves the root (git root, else CWD) and
-delegates the actual `.sah/` + `.prompts/` creation to
-`swissarmyhammer_workspace_init::ProjectStructure::new(root).init(...)`. The
-CLI keeps its own `deinit` (directory removal) and its mirdan-based
-`skill::SkillDeployment` (which targets detected coding-agent dirs â€” a distinct
-target from the workspace-local `.sah/skills/`, so not a fork). The `.sah/`
-workspace-structure logic now lives in exactly one place. CLI builds clean;
-all 451 CLI lib tests pass.
+### Finding 2 â€” `SkillDeployment` targets detected coding-agent dirs, not the board folder
+`SkillDeployment` deploys builtin skills into *detected coding-agent* directories (`.claude/skills/` etc.) via mirdan's global agent detection â€” not into a self-contained `.skills/` under the board folder that the kanban-app's in-process agent consumes. "Skills present in the board folder" is not what this component straightforwardly produces.

-### kanban-app wiring
-`apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs`: new `ensure_sah_workspace(&Path)` helper is
-called at the top of `BoardHandle::open` (reached from `AppState::open_board`).
-It roots `run_workspace_init` at the board folder (the parent of the `.kanban`
-directory), at `InitScope::Project`, on every board open. Idempotent; errors
-are logged and swallowed so a board still opens on a filesystem hiccup. The
-process CWD is never touched.
+### Library-access analysis (the delegated CLI-vs-crate choice)
+- `register_all` IS reachable as a library API: `swissarmyhammer-cli` has a `lib.rs` with `pub mod commands`, so `swissarmyhammer_cli::commands::registry::register_all` is public.
+- BUT depending on `swissarmyhammer-cli` drags in its full dependency tree into `kanban-app`: `llama-agent` (llama.cpp + Metal C++ build), `git2`, `rmcp`, `reqwest`, `swissarmyhammer-doctor`, `swissarmyhammer-lsp`, `swissarmyhammer-tools`. That is a large, inappropriate compile-time and binary-size burden for the desktop app.
+- Extracting the registry + components into a new library crate is a sizable refactor of CLI-private modules (`commands::registry`, `commands::install::components`, `commands::skill`) and still does not solve Finding 1 (the components remain CWD-rooted).

-### Tests added
-`apps/kanban-app/tests/workspace_init.rs` â€” 3 integration tests (written first,
-TDD): workspace + skills created on open; repeated open is idempotent (no
-duplication); init does not mutate process CWD. Plus 10 unit tests + 1 doctest
-in the new crate.
+### Decision needed from the user â€” pick one
+A. Add `swissarmyhammer-cli` as a dependency of `kanban-app` (accept the heavy dep tree), drive init by briefly setting process CWD to the board folder under a `CurrentDirGuard` + a global lock; accept the GUI-CWD-mutation race risk; only run the workspace + skills components (skip Claude-Code settings components).
+B. Extract the init registry + `Initializable` components into a new lightweight library crate, AND rework those components to accept an explicit root `&Path` instead of reading CWD/git-root. Larger scope; cleanest result; no CWD mutation. Likely needs its own kanban task.
+C. Narrow the kanban-app's in-process init to only what it actually needs â€” create the `.sah/` workspace dir + deploy builtin skills into a board-local `.skills/` â€” implemented directly against the lightweight `swissarmyhammer-common` (`SwissarmyhammerDirectory::from_custom_root`) and `swissarmyhammer-skills` (`SkillResolver`) crates, all rooted at an explicit board path with no CWD mutation and no heavy CLI dependency. Does not literally reuse `register_all`/the CLI components, but achieves the acceptance criteria (SAH dir + skills present, idempotent) cleanly.

-### Verification (all green)
-- `cargo build -p kanban-app` â€” clean
-- `cargo test -p kanban-app` â€” 126 tests pass (incl. 3 new integration tests)
-- `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-workspace-init` â€” 11 tests pass
-- `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli --lib` â€” 451 tests pass
-- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` â€” clean, zero warnings
\ No newline at end of file
+Recommendation: Option C. It satisfies every acceptance criterion, needs no process-CWD mutation, adds no heavy dependency, and uses crates the kanban-app can already reach. It does deviate from "reuse the exact `register_all` components," so it needs explicit approval before proceeding.
\ No newline at end of file

 integration tests, and clippy were all re-run locally and pass.

 ### Warnings
-- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/tests/workspace_init.rs:30-123` â€” All three integration tests call `run_workspace_init` directly; none exercise the actual production wiring (`ensure_sah_workspace` invoked inside `BoardHandle::open`, including the `kanban_path.parent()` board-folder math). If `ensure_sah_workspace` were removed from `BoardHandle::open`, or the `.parent()` path logic regressed, every test in this file would still pass. The acceptance criterion "opening a board folder makes it a SAH workspace" is verified only at the library layer. Add at least one test that opens a board via `AppState::open_board` (or `BoardHandle::open`) against a temp board dir and asserts `<board>/.sah/skills/plan/SKILL.md` exists â€” `state.rs` already has a `create_board_at` helper and `open_board`-based tests to model it on.
\ No newline at end of file
+- [x] `apps/kanban-app/tests/workspace_init.rs:30-123` â€” All three integration tests call `run_workspace_init` directly; none exercise the actual production wiring (`ensure_sah_workspace` invoked inside `BoardHandle::open`, including the `kanban_path.parent()` board-folder math). If `ensure_sah_workspace` were removed from `BoardHandle::open`, or the `.parent()` path logic regressed, every test in this file would still pass. The acceptance criterion "opening a board folder makes it a SAH workspace" is verified only at the library layer. Add at least one test that opens a board via `AppState::open_board` (or `BoardHandle::open`) against a temp board dir and asserts `<board>/.sah/skills/plan/SKILL.md` exists â€” `state.rs` already has a `create_board_at` helper and `open_board`-based tests to model it on.
+
+#### Resolution (2026-05-16)
+`kanban-app` is a binary-only crate (`[[bin]]`, no `lib.rs`); `state.rs` is a
+private `mod` and `AppState` / `BoardHandle` are `pub(crate)`, so an external
+integration test under `tests/` structurally cannot reach `open_board`. That is
+exactly why the existing `tests/workspace_init.rs` tests call the public
+`run_workspace_init` dependency API. The production-wiring test must therefore
+live in the in-crate `state.rs::tests` module â€” the only place that can drive
+`AppState::open_board` â†’ `BoardHandle::open` â†’ `ensure_sah_workspace` (and the
+finding itself points there: "`state.rs` already has a `create_board_at` helper
+and `open_board`-based tests to model it on").
+
+Added `state::tests::test_open_board_creates_sah_workspace_at_board_folder`: it
+calls `AppState::open_board(tmp.path(), None)` against a fresh temp board folder
+and asserts `<board>/.sah/skills/plan/SKILL.md` is a file, plus that `.sah/` is
+NOT nested inside `.kanban/`. This drives the real production path with no
+direct `run_workspace_init` call. Proven to genuinely fail on regression:
+commenting out `ensure_sah_workspace` in `BoardHandle::open` â†’ test fails
+(`.sah/` missing); changing the `kanban_path.parent()` math to root at
+`kanban_path` itself â†’ test fails (`.sah/` lands inside `.kanban/`).
+`cargo build -p kanban-app`, `cargo test -p kanban-app` (lib unit tests 102â†’103,
+all integration binaries green), and `cargo clippy -p kanban-app --all-targets
+-- -D warnings` all clean.
\ No newline at end of file
